salience.category: AI meal planner + auto-ordering for delivery
salience.core_problem: Daily food decisions, diet drift, and unaccountable delivery spend
salience.simple_description: Rasa plans your month of meals to your macros and budget, and orders them a tap at a time.

points_of_parity: Orders real food from real restaurants via Swiggy
Coupons and offers applied at checkout
points_of_difference: Plans a full 30 days ahead, not one order at a time
Hits estimated calorie and macro targets across the day
Enforces a hard monthly budget cap with per-meal envelopes
Cross-platform by design (Swiggy now; Zomato, Instamart, tiffin next)
performance.value_logic: Convenience gets you in the door; macro adherence and a spend cap are what make Rasa worth paying for.

usage_scenes: A noon confirm tap between meetings
imagery.mode_of_use: Set preferences once; approve the plan; tap to confirm each meal; otherwise forget it.

judgments.credibility_proof: One-tap gates, allergen hard-blocks, a visible spend ledger, and honest 'estimated' macro labels.

feelings.voice: A calm, competent friend who has your meals handled
feelings.tone: Warm and pragmatic — never preachy, never a diet scold

resonance.loyalty_logic: The preference-and-macro graph gets smarter every meal, so leaving means starting over.
resonance.mission: Give people back the daily hour and headspace that food decisions quietly steal.
resonance.community_connection: For people who order in daily and want it to finally be smart, balanced, and within budget.
